keyRules: Maximum 120 m above ground level (EASA Open category)
Register as a UAS operator (€25/year, or €10 for a tourist pass)
A1/A3 online training required — minimum age 16
Third-party insurance mandatory for drones of any weight
The ENTIRE country is controlled airspace — always check IDRONECT
Always keep the drone within visual line of sight (VLOS)
Do not fly over assemblies of people. Rules for uninvolved persons depend on subcategory, class mark and operating conditions; check the official matrix.
The Malta International Airport CTR covers most of the island
Night flying prohibited without specific authorisation
Valletta and historic areas: additional restrictions apply
importantNotes: The ENTIRE country is controlled airspace — unique in the EU
Insurance mandatory for drones of any weight
The Malta Airport CTR covers most of the main island
Tourist registration available (€10) for short-term visitors
GDPR applies when capturing images of identifiable people

fields.disclaimerText: Always check the official TM-CAD sources and IDRONECT before flying. This app is a tool — not a legal authority. Malta follows EASA regulations — note that the entire country is controlled airspace.
